prompt: Search X + web for the NEWEST 2026 entrants (last 90 days) in AI-agent security, AI trust/attestation, and compliance-for-AI — who just launched or raised, and could fast-follow an 'agent security review readiness attestation' product? Cite.
content:
**Several early-stage companies in AI-agent security, AI governance/trust/attestation, and AI compliance have launched or raised funding in the last ~90 days (roughly since early March 2026, given the June 7, 2026 query date).** These focus on securing/governing autonomous AI agents, runtime protections, red-teaming/guardrails, identity/access for agents, or automating compliance/risk workflows—positioning them well to extend into or fast-follow products like “agent security review readiness attestation” (e.g., verifiable assessments of agent posture, controls, compliance, and trustworthiness).[[1]](https://fortune.com/2026/05/28/geordie-security-governance-ai-agents/)[[2]](https://www.bankinfosecurity.com/surf-raises-57m-to-automate-security-hygiene-ai-agents-a-31039)

Key recent examples (prioritized by recency where possible; details drawn from funding announcements and coverage):

- **Geordie AI** (London): Raised $30M Series A (May 28, 2026; total ~$36.5M) led by Balderton Capital. Provides a security and governance platform positioned as “air traffic control” for enterprise AI agents, covering posture, observability, and compliance. Emerged from stealth with seed in Sept 2025. Strong fit for governance/attestation extensions.[[1]](https://fortune.com/2026/05/28/geordie-security-governance-ai-agents/)[[1]](https://fortune.com/2026/05/28/geordie-security-governance-ai-agents/)

- **Exponential Security Labs** (Tübingen, Germany): Newly announced startup (around early June 2026) focused on securing agentic AI systems via self-improving red-teaming and guardrail agents. Backed by AI safety researchers; targets reliability/security for high-stakes autonomous agents. Directly relevant to agent security review and attestation-style verification.[[3]](https://x.com/maksym_andr/status/2061462625175912908)

- **Greenboard**: Raised $15.5M Series A (May 12, 2026; following 2024 seed). AI-powered platform automating financial compliance processes (including conversational tools). Applicable to compliance-for-AI in regulated sectors.[[4]](https://fortune.com/2026/05/12/greenboard-raises-15-5-million-series-a-to-keep-compliance-from-slowing-down-business/)

- **Variance**: Raised $21.5M Series A (April 7, 2026). Builds agentic AI tools specifically for risk and compliance workflows, targeting financial institutions and enterprises. Ideal for compliance-for-AI agent products.[[5]](https://regtechanalyst.com/variance-bags-21-5m-to-power-ai-compliance-agents/)

- **Trent AI** (London): Raised €11M (~$13M) Seed (April 7, 2026) as it emerged from stealth. Offers layered security solutions built for the agentic era.[[6]](https://bebeez.eu/2026/04/07/with-74-of-businesses-planning-agentic-ai-deployment-trent-ai-secures-e11-million-in-seed-funding/)

- **Haast**: Raised $12M Series A (April 9, 2026). Focuses on AI compliance automation to reduce manual legal/review burdens for enterprises.[[7]](https://angelinvestorsnetwork.com/regulatory-compliance/ai-compliance-automation-raises-12m-series-a-in-2026)

- **Manifold**: Closed $8M Seed (March 22, 2026). Develops an AI Detection and Response (AIDR) platform for runtime security of autonomous AI agents in enterprise networks/endpoints.[[8]](https://i-hls.com/archives/135449)

- **Surf AI** (New York): Launched from stealth with $57M funding (March 17, 2026; led by Accel). Agentic operations platform using AI agents for automated security hygiene, exposure remediation, and security ops across identities/cloud/data.[[2]](https://www.bankinfosecurity.com/surf-raises-57m-to-automate-security-hygiene-ai-agents-a-31039)[[9]](https://siliconangle.com/2026/03/17/surf-ai-launches-agentic-security-operations-platform-57m-funding-round/)

- **Armadin**: Raised ~$190M (combined seed + Series A; March 10, 2026). Founded by Mandiant’s Kevin Mandia; builds autonomous AI-native cybersecurity agents for threat response.[[10]](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/10/mandiants-founder-just-raised-190m-for-his-autonomous-ai-agent-security-startup/)

**Additional context and notes**:
- Other mentions include Token Security (identity-first AI agent security; highlighted in May 2026 “Rising in Cyber” list) and various YC-backed or earlier players (e.g., Clearly AI’s Feb 2026 seed for product/AI deployment risk reviews, or Overmind’s Feb seed for AI agent supervision). Israeli startups have been notably active in agent security raises.[[11]](https://www.token.security/news/token-security-named-to-rising-in-cyber-2026-list-of-top-cybersecurity-startups)[[12]](https://www.geekwire.com/2026/seattle-security-startup-led-by-husband-wife-duo-raises-8-4m/)
- Broader ecosystem signals include growing focus on agent governance (e.g., Zenity summits, NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative), with funding flowing into security/governance tooling amid enterprise agent adoption.[[13]](https://zenity.io/resources/events/ai-agent-security-summit-2026)[[14]](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-nist-ai-agent-red-teaming-standards-202603/)
- These companies are early-stage and specialized, making them plausible fast-followers or collaborators for attestation/review products that verify agent security readiness, controls, and compliance posture.

X discussions echo the rapid category formation around “agent security” specifically (distinct from general cybersecurity), with recent startup announcements and compliance themes.[[15]](https://x.com/dovzhikova/status/2061710017439420683)

For the absolute latest or deeper due diligence, check Crunchbase/PitchBook or direct company sites, as the space is moving quickly.
